review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's intent to separate user and administrative functionalities using Kubernetes RBAC. The naming convention mostly follows the established pattern, but a slight adjustment was made to improve clarity and specificity. The functions accurately reflect the technical controls available in Kubernetes for managing role-based access.
naming_improvements: Changed 'roles' to 'functionality' in k8s_rbac_separate_user_and_admin_roles for better alignment with the control's focus on functionality separation
title: 3_13_3: 3.13.3 Separate user functionality from system management functionality
step1_reasoning: The control requires separating user functionality from system management functionality, which directly maps to Kubernetes RBAC practices. In Kubernetes, this can be achieved by ensuring that users do not have cluster-admin privileges unless necessary, separating roles for regular users and administrators, and enforcing least privilege principles. These checks ensure that administrative tasks are not performed by regular users, aligning with the control's intent.
